steps.title: Focus on the audience
steps.content: The audience is already familiar with the MBC Project, so the sentence should not spend its energy explaining MBC's mission. It needs to use the notes to introduce Clarence Theodore Ward as the person whose career will be discussed next. The best setup should make Ward the subject, not the broader directory, while still showing why MBC is relevant.

steps.title: Eliminate unfocused choices
steps.content:
• Choice A shifts away from Ward by saying there are many more composers in the directory.
  • Choice B gives only Ward's birth and death years, which is too narrow to launch a career discussion.
  • Choice D re-explains the directory's purpose and treats Ward only as an example.

steps.title: Choose the career setup
steps.content: Choice C identifies Ward as a classical music composer included in MBC, which connects him to the familiar project without repeating background the audience already knows. It also keeps Ward, not the project as a whole, at the center of the sentence for the discussion ahead. The answer is C.
